item.title: Thank you =)
item.content:encoded:
Hey!

Thank you so much for your comment! I tried to make it inclusive through the writing and didn’t really have that pointed out so I appreciate you pointing that out! I got some advice from other authors and have changed/reworded the sections you brought up! If it’s still an issue please let me know!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My only real criticism on this is the use of the terminology "biological sex," especially since a young child wouldn't have any concept of such a thing.

Perhaps refer instead to gender? I feel like that would not only make more sense in the context of the article, it would be more inclusive, and more scientifically sound. =)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love this! It's cute, it has an edge of comedy, and it is pretty clever retake of Sigurros/239 kinda stuff but with less existential horror that ended up making it kinda too big to really care about.

Nonetheless, this article scared the shit out of me.

It reminded me how much environment shapes a child's, and even an adult's, perception of the world. I had always been particularly easy to sway as a kid, I can't count how many times I cried to my parents for snacks I saw on television and I'm sure I at least doubled their electric bills by insisting that I won't eat cold rice and forcing them to keep our rice warm 24/7. To even consider that the same "innocent" faith in honesty could shift and transform the real world, and hurts others…

+1, and not only because it's a gift.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a really clever take on the prompt, and a really clever skip in general.

The dialogue is well written. The ending is great, and very much adheres to the previously established rules, avoiding a deus ex machina vibe.

+1.

item.title: Clever, thorough, amusing
item.content:encoded: Leave it to a reality bender in grade school, making cooties into a legitimate childhood disease…and if this disease acts as children expect, of course the mock-medical ritual for prevention will work too. Bravo!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My review from the Art Exchange forum:

This is a skip about cooties. It's… actually really, really clever. I don't think I want to spoil it. It's strangely cute, rather funny, and wildly clever. Please read it. Great gift. Oh, and I just saw the URL, and what a pun, wow.

Looks like you have made said pun into the title, and while puns can never be truly, 100%, unironically enjoyed, I can not blame you. My opinion hasn't changed since writing that review, either. This is clever. Never thought I'd see cooties made into an SCP, and also never thought that the execution could be this good on such a concept. I love it to bits. +1 with gusto.

item.title: Author post!
item.content:encoded:
How do you write about an infectious disease in a global pandemic? Apparently you just have to make it cute!

I had a lot of fun writing this SCiP for Rigen for the holiday exchange! When I first got the prompt of making “art or tale about anomalous disease SCP/SCPs" I was really unsure of what to do since I couldn't figure out a way to do a tale about a disease without paralleling the current pandemic (and I honestly didn't realize that the request was for art or tale rather than an SCP until like 2 days before the due date). But I think what came out is fun and people have said it’s funny, cute, and really clever so I guess others agree! Happy Holidays everyone!

Writing Critters: Orgath, Dr. Whitney, Grigori Karpin, HarryBlank, Dysadron, Dr. Chandra, RadioactiveRADs, Radguel, Phiiota, Ralliston,

Science Critters: Opal and Solari

Protocol art: TheRiceandCake
